{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development (Fintech)",
  "description": "Easy Buy Limited&nbsp;- We are a fast-growing financial services organization operating under a duly licensed finance company regulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN). Since our establishment in 2019, the company has built a strong reputation for delivering innovative and accessible financial solutions that empower individuals and businesses across Nigeria.&nbsp;Through one of our dynamic consumer-focused platforms, we provide flexible financing solutions, including asset financing and other customer-centric financial products designed to improve access to essential goods and services. Our solutions are particularly tailored to meet the needs of underserved and emerging markets, helping customers achieve their goals through convenient and structured payment plans.

Backed by a team of forward-thinking financial professionals and industry experts, the organization combines deep market insight with strong financial expertise to design tailor-made lending, investment, and financial advisory solutions for clients across both the private and public sectors.As we continue to expand our footprint and impact, we remain committed to innovation, integrity, and financial inclusion, providing opportunities for talented professionals who are passionate about growth, performance, and making a meaningful difference in the financial services landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development (Fintech)

Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Drive sales growth by ensuringtargets are met.
	Mobilize new customers and grow assigned territories.
	Conduct consistent follow-up on clients to ensure low default rate.
	Meet and exceed assigned weekly and monthly sales targets.
	Maintain accurate records of customer accounts and repayment schedules
	Build and sustain strong customer relationships within assigned communities


Requirements


	OND / HND / B.Sc. in any relevant field.
	Minimum of 1 &ndash; 2 years of experience in Sales, Field Marketing, or Direct Sales (fintech experience is an advantage)
	Strong persuasion, negotiation, and interpersonal skills
	Target-driven with proven sales performance record
	Basic reporting and documentation skills.


Benefits


	Salary:&nbsp;N150,000 monthly.
	Performance Bonus: Up to 150,000 Monthly.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;humanresources.prisc@outlook.com&nbsp;using &quot;Business Developer &ndash; Ibadan&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Business Development (Fintech) Job at Easy Buy Limited




        
    

        
        Easy Buy Limited - We are a fast-growing financial services organization operating under a duly licensed finance company regulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN). Since our establishment in 2019, the company has built a strong reputation for delivering innovative and accessible financial solutions that empower individuals and businesses across Nigeria. Through one of our dynamic consumer-focused platforms, we provide flexible financing solutions, including asset financing and other customer-centric financial products designed to improve access to essential goods and services. Our solutions are particularly tailored to meet the needs of underserved and emerging markets, helping customers achieve their goals through convenient and structured payment plans.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Backed by a team of forward-thinking financial professionals and industry experts, the organization combines deep market insight with strong financial expertise to design tailor-made lending, investment, and financial advisory solutions for clients across both the private and public sectors.As we continue to expand our footprint and impact, we remain committed to innovation, integrity, and financial inclusion, providing opportunities for talented professionals who are passionate about growth, performance, and making a meaningful difference in the financial services landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development (Fintech)

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Drive sales growth by ensuringtargets are met.
	Mobilize new customers and grow assigned territories.
	Conduct consistent follow-up on clients to ensure low default rate.
	Meet and exceed assigned weekly and monthly sales targets.
	Maintain accurate records of customer accounts and repayment schedules
	Build and sustain strong customer relationships within assigned communities


Requirements


	OND / HND / B.Sc. in any relevant field.
	Minimum of 1 – 2 years of experience in Sales, Field Marketing, or Direct Sales (fintech experience is an advantage)
	Strong persuasion, negotiation, and interpersonal skills
	Target-driven with proven sales performance record
	Basic reporting and documentation skills.


Benefits


	Salary: N150,000 monthly.
	Performance Bonus: Up to 150,000 Monthly.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Business Developer – Ibadan" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Key Account Manager (Healthtech Logistics)",
  "description": "LifeBank is a technology company that powers hospitals and care centers to deliver quality healthcare to patients in emerging markets. The company&rsquo;s technology, infrastructure, agile production, and distribution networks enable hospitals in emerging markets to catch up with their global peers. The company has distributed 45,000+ medical products to 1000+ healthcare facilities, saving 18,000+ lives across Nigeria, Ethiopia and Kenya. At LifeBank, we are the technology-led healthcare system for the future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Key Account Manager (Healthtech Logistics)
Job Location:&nbsp;Onipan, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports to:Director, Customer Health

Job Summary


	The Key Account Manager will be responsible for managing and growing strategic healthcare and institutional accounts across hospitals, labs, blood banks, and partner facilities.
	The role ensures seamless service delivery, drives revenue growth, strengthens long-term client relationships, and supports life-critical logistics operations that improve patient outcomes.
	This role sits at the intersection of healthcare, logistics, and commercial growth, requiring strong relationship management, operational coordination, and a deep understanding of healthcare delivery ecosystems.


Key Responsibilities
Key Account Management:


	Manage a portfolio of hospitals, diagnostics centers, and healthcare institutions
	Serve as the primary point of contact for all assigned key accounts
	Ensure consistent service delivery and rapid resolution of client issues


Revenue Growth &amp; Retention:


	Drive revenue growth within existing accounts through upselling and cross-selling of services (e.g., logistics, supply chain solutions, emergency fulfillment)
	Achieve monthly/quarterly revenue and retention targets
	Identify new opportunities within assigned accounts and convert them into active business


Client Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with hospital administrators, procurement teams, and clinical stakeholders
	Conduct regular business reviews to assess performance, satisfaction, and service improvement opportunities
	Act as a trusted advisor to clients on supply chain reliability and emergency healthcare logistics


Operational Coordination:


	Liaise with operations, dispatch, pharmacy, lab, and supply chain teams to ensure timely fulfillment of client requests
	Monitor order fulfillment performance and escalate delays or service gaps proactively
	Ensure accurate documentation and compliance with healthcare logistics protocols


Account Strategy &amp; Planning:


	Develop structured account plans for each key client
	Forecast demand and support operational planning for critical supplies (blood, oxygen, medical products, etc.)
	Align account strategies with company growth and impact goals


Data, Reporting &amp; CRM Management:


	Maintain accurate records of all client interactions in CRM systems
	Track key account performance metrics (order volume, fulfillment time, revenue contribution)
	Provide regular reports on account health and growth opportunities


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Key account retention rate
	Revenue growth per account
	Service level performance (on-time delivery, fulfillment success rate)
	Client satisfaction (CSAT/NPS)
	Number of upsell/cross-sell conversions
	Responsiveness to client escalations


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration, Public Health, Supply Chain, or related field


Experience:


	4&ndash;6 years in Key Account Management, Sales, or Client Relationship roles
	Experience in healthcare, logistics, pharmaceuticals, or B2B enterprise services strongly preferred
	Familiarity with hospital procurement systems is an advantage


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong relationship management and stakeholder engagement skills
	Excellent communication and negotiation abilities
	Strong commercial awareness and revenue ownership mindset
	Ability to work in fast-paced, high-pressure environments
	Strong coordination and problem-solving skills across multiple teams
	Proficiency in CRM tools (Salesforce, HubSpot, or similar)


Personal Attributes:


	High sense of urgency (critical in healthcare logistics)
	Strong ownership mindset
	Detail-oriented and highly organized
	Empathetic and client-focused
	Calm and effective under pressure
	Passionate about improving healthcare outcomes


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 monthly.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Resume to:&nbsp;eam@lifebank.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-10",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

Key Account Manager (Healthtech Logistics) Job at LifeBank




        
    

        
        LifeBank is a technology company that powers hospitals and care centers to deliver quality healthcare to patients in emerging markets. The company’s technology, infrastructure, agile production, and distribution networks enable hospitals in emerging markets to catch up with their global peers. The company has distributed 45,000+ medical products to 1000+ healthcare facilities, saving 18,000+ lives across Nigeria, Ethiopia and Kenya. At LifeBank, we are the technology-led healthcare system for the future.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Key Account Manager (Healthtech Logistics)
Job Location: Onipan, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports to:Director, Customer Health

Job Summary


	The Key Account Manager will be responsible for managing and growing strategic healthcare and institutional accounts across hospitals, labs, blood banks, and partner facilities.
	The role ensures seamless service delivery, drives revenue growth, strengthens long-term client relationships, and supports life-critical logistics operations that improve patient outcomes.
	This role sits at the intersection of healthcare, logistics, and commercial growth, requiring strong relationship management, operational coordination, and a deep understanding of healthcare delivery ecosystems.


Key Responsibilities
Key Account Management:


	Manage a portfolio of hospitals, diagnostics centers, and healthcare institutions
	Serve as the primary point of contact for all assigned key accounts
	Ensure consistent service delivery and rapid resolution of client issues


Revenue Growth & Retention:


	Drive revenue growth within existing accounts through upselling and cross-selling of services (e.g., logistics, supply chain solutions, emergency fulfillment)
	Achieve monthly/quarterly revenue and retention targets
	Identify new opportunities within assigned accounts and convert them into active business


Client Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with hospital administrators, procurement teams, and clinical stakeholders
	Conduct regular business reviews to assess performance, satisfaction, and service improvement opportunities
	Act as a trusted advisor to clients on supply chain reliability and emergency healthcare logistics


Operational Coordination:


	Liaise with operations, dispatch, pharmacy, lab, and supply chain teams to ensure timely fulfillment of client requests
	Monitor order fulfillment performance and escalate delays or service gaps proactively
	Ensure accurate documentation and compliance with healthcare logistics protocols


Account Strategy & Planning:


	Develop structured account plans for each key client
	Forecast demand and support operational planning for critical supplies (blood, oxygen, medical products, etc.)
	Align account strategies with company growth and impact goals


Data, Reporting & CRM Management:


	Maintain accurate records of all client interactions in CRM systems
	Track key account performance metrics (order volume, fulfillment time, revenue contribution)
	Provide regular reports on account health and growth opportunities


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Key account retention rate
	Revenue growth per account
	Service level performance (on-time delivery, fulfillment success rate)
	Client satisfaction (CSAT/NPS)
	Number of upsell/cross-sell conversions
	Responsiveness to client escalations


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Public Health, Supply Chain, or related field


Experience:


	4–6 years in Key Account Management, Sales, or Client Relationship roles
	Experience in healthcare, logistics, pharmaceuticals, or B2B enterprise services strongly preferred
	Familiarity with hospital procurement systems is an advantage


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong relationship management and stakeholder engagement skills
	Excellent communication and negotiation abilities
	Strong commercial awareness and revenue ownership mindset
	Ability to work in fast-paced, high-pressure environments
	Strong coordination and problem-solving skills across multiple teams
	Proficiency in CRM tools (Salesforce, HubSpot, or similar)


Personal Attributes:


	High sense of urgency (critical in healthcare logistics)
	Strong ownership mindset
	Detail-oriented and highly organized
	Empathetic and client-focused
	Calm and effective under pressure
	Passionate about improving healthcare outcomes


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 monthly. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Manager",
  "description": "Easy Buy Limited is a fast-growing financial services organization operating under a duly licensed finance company regulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN). Since our establishment in 2019, the company has built a strong reputation for delivering innovative and accessible financial solutions that empower individuals and businesses across Nigeria.

Through one of our dynamic consumer-focused platforms, we provide flexible financing solutions, including asset financing and other customer-centric financial products designed to improve access to essential goods and services. Our solutions are particularly tailored to meet the needs of underserved and emerging markets, helping customers achieve their goals through convenient and structured payment plans.

Backed by a team of forward-thinking financial professionals and industry experts, the organization combines deep market insight with strong financial expertise to design tailor-made lending, investment, and financial advisory solutions for clients across both the private and public sectors.

As we continue to expand our footprint and impact, we remain committed to innovation, integrity, and financial inclusion, providing opportunities for talented professionals who are passionate about growth, performance, and making a meaningful difference in the financial services landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily operational activities to ensure efficiency and compliance with company policies
	Drive sales growth by ensuring revenue targets are met and exceeded within the region.
	Ensure alignment of all Business Development teams with company goals, productivity standards, and performance targets.
	Coordinate operational support for sales and business development teams.
	Monitor operational performance and implement improvements where necessary
	Collaborate with regional and sales leadership to achieve business targets
	Identify operational risks and implement corrective actions


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in Sales, Marketing, Business Administration, or related field
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in operations management, preferably within fintech, sales-driven, or financial services organizations
	Strong organizational and problem-solving skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft tools.
	Ability to manage multiple teams and operational processes effectively.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary
	Career growth opportunities
	Supportive and performance-driven team environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;humanresources.prisc@outlook.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Operations Manager Job at Easy Buy Limited




        
    

        
        Easy Buy Limited is a fast-growing financial services organization operating under a duly licensed finance company regulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN). Since our establishment in 2019, the company has built a strong reputation for delivering innovative and accessible financial solutions that empower individuals and businesses across Nigeria.

Through one of our dynamic consumer-focused platforms, we provide flexible financing solutions, including asset financing and other customer-centric financial products designed to improve access to essential goods and services. Our solutions are particularly tailored to meet the needs of underserved and emerging markets, helping customers achieve their goals through convenient and structured payment plans.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Backed by a team of forward-thinking financial professionals and industry experts, the organization combines deep market insight with strong financial expertise to design tailor-made lending, investment, and financial advisory solutions for clients across both the private and public sectors.

As we continue to expand our footprint and impact, we remain committed to innovation, integrity, and financial inclusion, providing opportunities for talented professionals who are passionate about growth, performance, and making a meaningful difference in the financial services landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily operational activities to ensure efficiency and compliance with company policies
	Drive sales growth by ensuring revenue targets are met and exceeded within the region.
	Ensure alignment of all Business Development teams with company goals, productivity standards, and performance targets.
	Coordinate operational support for sales and business development teams.
	Monitor operational performance and implement improvements where necessary
	Collaborate with regional and sales leadership to achieve business targets
	Identify operational risks and implement corrective actions


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree in Sales, Marketing, Business Administration, or related field
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in operations management, preferably within fintech, sales-driven, or financial services organizations
	Strong organizational and problem-solving skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft tools.
	Ability to manage multiple teams and operational processes effectively.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary
	Career growth opportunities
	Supportive and performance-driven team environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Agent (Hausa Speaking)",
  "description": "LifeBank is a technology company that powers hospitals and care centers to deliver quality healthcare to patients in emerging markets. The company&rsquo;s technology, infrastructure, agile production, and distribution networks enable hospitals in emerging markets to catch up with their global peers. The company has distributed 45,000+ medical products to 1000+ healthcare facilities, saving 18,000+ lives across Nigeria, Ethiopia and Kenya. At LifeBank, we are the technology-led healthcare system for the future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Agent (Hausa Speaking)

Job Location:&nbsp;Bodija, Ibadan - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Customer Operations / Support
Reports to:Director of Operations

Role Summary


	The Customer Service Agent (Hausa Speaking) is responsible for providing excellent customer support to healthcare facilities, hospitals, and partners across Nigeria, with a focus on Hausa-speaking regions.
	The role ensures timely resolution of inquiries, order support, and effective communication between customers and internal operations teams.
	You will act as a key bridge between customers and LifeBank&rsquo;s logistics and supply chain teams, ensuring seamless service delivery and customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
Customer Support &amp; Communication:


	Attend to customer inquiries via phone, WhatsApp, email, and chat in both Hausa and English.
	Provide clear information on product availability, order status, and delivery timelines.
	Resolve customer complaints promptly and professionally.
	Escalate complex issues to the appropriate internal teams when necessary.


Order &amp; Service Coordination:


	Track and update customers on orders in real-time.
	Coordinate with logistics and operations teams to ensure timely delivery of medical supplies.
	Confirm order accuracy and support order modifications where required.
	Ensure proper documentation of all customer interactions.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Build strong relationships with hospitals, clinics, and healthcare partners.
	Ensure high levels of customer satisfaction and retention.
	Proactively follow up with customers on pending or delayed requests.


Data &amp; Reporting:


	Log all customer interactions in the CRM system accurately.
	Maintain daily reports on customer issues, resolution status, and recurring challenges.
	Provide feedback to improve service delivery processes.


Compliance &amp; Quality Standards:


	Adhere to company service standards and healthcare logistics protocols.
	Ensure confidentiality of customer and patient-related information.
	Follow escalation procedures for critical or emergency requests.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Customer satisfaction score (CSAT)
	Average response and resolution time
	Number of successfully resolved tickets
	Accuracy of order tracking and updates
	Complaint escalation rate
	Customer retention and repeat engagement


What Success Looks Like


	Customers receive timely, accurate, and empathetic support.
	Reduced service delays through effective coordination.
	Strong trust and satisfaction from healthcare partners in Hausa-speaking regions.
	Smooth communication flow between customers and internal teams.


Key Requirements
Education &amp; Experience:


	Diploma or Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in any relevant field.
	2&ndash;3 years of experience in customer service, call center, or operations support (healthcare/logistics preferred).


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Fluency in Hausa and English (mandatory).
	Strong communication and active listening skills.
	High emotional intelligence and patience in handling complaints.
	Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment.
	Basic computer literacy and familiarity with CRM tools.
	Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.


Salary
N120,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Resume to:&nbsp;eam@lifebank.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Agent (Hausa Speaking) Job at LifeBank




        
    

        
        LifeBank is a technology company that powers hospitals and care centers to deliver quality healthcare to patients in emerging markets. The company’s technology, infrastructure, agile production, and distribution networks enable hospitals in emerging markets to catch up with their global peers. The company has distributed 45,000+ medical products to 1000+ healthcare facilities, saving 18,000+ lives across Nigeria, Ethiopia and Kenya. At LifeBank, we are the technology-led healthcare system for the future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Agent (Hausa Speaking)
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Bodija, Ibadan - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Customer Operations / Support
Reports to:Director of Operations

Role Summary


	The Customer Service Agent (Hausa Speaking) is responsible for providing excellent customer support to healthcare facilities, hospitals, and partners across Nigeria, with a focus on Hausa-speaking regions.
	The role ensures timely resolution of inquiries, order support, and effective communication between customers and internal operations teams.
	You will act as a key bridge between customers and LifeBank’s logistics and supply chain teams, ensuring seamless service delivery and customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
Customer Support & Communication:


	Attend to customer inquiries via phone, WhatsApp, email, and chat in both Hausa and English.
	Provide clear information on product availability, order status, and delivery timelines.
	Resolve customer complaints promptly and professionally.
	Escalate complex issues to the appropriate internal teams when necessary.


Order & Service Coordination:


	Track and update customers on orders in real-time.
	Coordinate with logistics and operations teams to ensure timely delivery of medical supplies.
	Confirm order accuracy and support order modifications where required.
	Ensure proper documentation of all customer interactions.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Build strong relationships with hospitals, clinics, and healthcare partners.
	Ensure high levels of customer satisfaction and retention.
	Proactively follow up with customers on pending or delayed requests.


Data & Reporting:


	Log all customer interactions in the CRM system accurately.
	Maintain daily reports on customer issues, resolution status, and recurring challenges.
	Provide feedback to improve service delivery processes.


Compliance & Quality Standards:


	Adhere to company service standards and healthcare logistics protocols.
	Ensure confidentiality of customer and patient-related information.
	Follow escalation procedures for critical or emergency requests.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Customer satisfaction score (CSAT)
	Average response and resolution time
	Number of successfully resolved tickets
	Accuracy of order tracking and updates
	Complaint escalation rate
	Customer retention and repeat engagement


What Success Looks Like


	Customers receive timely, accurate, and empathetic support.
	Reduced service delays through effective coordination.
	Strong trust and satisfaction from healthcare partners in Hausa-speaking regions.
	Smooth communication flow between customers and internal teams.


Key Requirements
Education & Experience:


	Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in any relevant field.
	2–3 years of experience in customer service, call center, or operations support (healthcare/logistics preferred).


Skills & Competencies:


	Fluency in Hausa and English (mandatory).
	Strong communication and active listening skills.
	High emotional intelligence and patience in handling complaints.
	Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment.
	Basic computer literacy and familiarity with CRM tools.
	Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.


Salary
N120,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "title": "Internal Control Associate",
  "description": "CarePoint Hospitals is a trusted healthcare provider committed to delivering quality, patient-centered care through professional excellence, modern systems, and a supportive work environment that promotes continuous improvement and teamwork.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Internal Control Associate

Job Location:&nbsp;Egbeda, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Internal Control Officer to strengthen internal processes, ensure compliance, and mitigate operational and financial risks across the organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, review, and implement internal control systems and policies
	Monitor compliance with financial regulations and organizational procedures
	Conduct periodic audits and risk assessments
	Identify control gaps and recommend improvements
	Prepare detailed reports on audit findings and control issues
	Ensure proper documentation of financial and operational processes
	Support management in enforcing accountability across departments


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field
	2&ndash;6 years of experience in internal control, auditing, or compliance
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	Good knowledge of risk management and financial regulations
	Excellent communication and reporting skills
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and attention to detail


Work Schedule:


	Monday &ndash; Friday: 8:00 AM &ndash; 5:00 PM
	Alternate Saturdays: 9:00 AM &ndash; 2:00 PM.


Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Professional development opportunities
	Pension and leave benefits
	Healthcare coverage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and credentials to:&nbsp;hr@carepointhospitals.com&nbsp;using&nbsp;&quot;Application &ndash; Internal Control Officer&quot;&nbsp;as the subject of the email.",

Internal Control Associate Job at CarePoint Hospitals




        
    

        
        CarePoint Hospitals is a trusted healthcare provider committed to delivering quality, patient-centered care through professional excellence, modern systems, and a supportive work environment that promotes continuous improvement and teamwork.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Associate

Job Location: Egbeda, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Internal Control Officer to strengthen internal processes, ensure compliance, and mitigate operational and financial risks across the organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, review, and implement internal control systems and policies
	Monitor compliance with financial regulations and organizational procedures
	Conduct periodic audits and risk assessments
	Identify control gaps and recommend improvements
	Prepare detailed reports on audit findings and control issues
	Ensure proper documentation of financial and operational processes
	Support management in enforcing accountability across departments


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field
	2–6 years of experience in internal control, auditing, or compliance
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	Good knowledge of risk management and financial regulations
	Excellent communication and reporting skills
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and attention to detail


Work Schedule:


	Monday – Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM
	Alternate Saturdays: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM.


Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Professional development opportunities
	Pension and leave benefits
	Healthcare coverage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and credentials to: [email protected] using "Application – Internal Control Officer" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
